title: 10 best herbal tinctures for over bleeding during periods
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for over bleeding during periods are liquid extracts made from plants, used to alleviate excessive menstrual flow. These tinctures work by regulating hormonal imbalances and strengthening uterine contractions, reducing blood loss and discomfort. For example, the tincture of Cramp Bark (Viburnum opulus) helps to reduce cramps and spasms, while Chamomile (Matricaria chamomilla) soothes and calms the uterus. By using these herbal remedies, women can experience improved menstrual management, reduced anxiety and fatigue, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to live a more comfortable and empowered life during their periods.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce uterine contractions that can cause excessive bleeding. The active compound curcumin in turmeric also has natural platelet aggregation inhibitors, which prevent blood clots from forming and reduce the risk of heavy bleeding. Additionally, turmeric's antiseptic and astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and promote clotting, further reducing menstrual bleeding.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compound curcumin helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heavy menstrual bleeding, which can contribute to reduced bleeding.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress that can damage the uterine lining and lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Platelet-aggregating property: Turmeric contains a compound called kaempferol, which helps to increase platelet aggregation, thereby improving blood clotting and reducing the likelihood of heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin helps with over bleeding during periods by exerting anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties, reducing the release of prostaglandins and other chemicals that stimulate uterine contractions and bleeding.

Turmerones: Turmerones, a class of sesquiterpenes, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to reduce bleeding by inhibiting the production of inflammatory mediators and promoting the healing of damaged tissues in the uterus.

Ferulic acid: Ferulic acid, a phenolic compound, has been shown to have a potential role in reducing menstrual bleeding by inhibiting the activity of prostaglandins and other chemicals that stimulate uterine contractions and bleeding.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are used due to their high concentration of bioactive compounds like curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory and anticoagulant properties that help alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
Roots: They are used for their emmenagogic properties, which help to stimulate uterine contractions and reduce bleeding during periods.
Leaves: They are used for their ability to help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the curcuma longa roots at the end of the growing season when the tops have died back.
Clean and dry the harvested roots in a warm dry place for at least 2 weeks.
Chop the dried roots into small pieces and combine with 70 vodka in a ratio of 1 part root to 2 parts vodka.
Store the mixture in a glass container with a tight fitting lid in a cool dark place for 6 weeks.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its ability to tone and regulate uterine muscles. The tannins present in the herb help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive flow. Additionally, Maidenhair tree has been traditionally used to stop excessive menstruation due to its astringent properties. By taking a Maidenhair tree tincture supplement during periods, women can experience relief from heavy bleeding and cramping, promoting overall menstrual health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: The tannins and flavonoids in Maidenhair Tree Tincture have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation in the uterine lining and prevent excessive bleeding.
Vasoconstrictive Properties: Ginkgo biloba's active compounds, such as ginkgolic acids, can constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow to the uterus and helping to slow down or stop heavy bleeding.

Antifibrinolytic Properties: Maidenhair Tree Tincture contains compounds that inhibit fibrinolysis, a process that breaks down blood clots. By inhibiting this process, the tincture can help reduce excessive bleeding by preventing the breakdown of blood clots in the uterus.
remedy_constituents: Ginkgolides: Ginkgolides help reduce over bleeding during periods by inhibiting platelet-activating factor (PAF), a compound involved in blood clotting, thereby preventing excessive blood loss.

Bilobalide: Bilobalide has a protective effect on the endometrium, the lining of the uterus, which may help reduce bleeding during periods by promoting the growth of new endometrial cells and improving blood vessel function.

Flavonoids (including quercetin and kaempferol): Flavonoids in Ginkgo biloba tinctures help reduce inflammation and improve blood vessel function, which may contribute to a reduction in over bleeding during periods by promoting healthy blood vessel constriction and reducing menstrual flow.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Ginkgo biloba leaves are the most commonly used part for tinctures due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which help to stabilize blood vessels and reduce menstrual bleeding.
Seeds: Ginkgo biloba seeds are also used to make tinctures, as they contain ginkgolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that may help to alleviate menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.
Buds: Ginkgo biloba buds are occasionally used to make tinctures, as they contain flavonoids and terpenes that may help to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of ginkgo biloba leaves and 1 part of ginkgo biloba seeds and dry them for 2 weeks.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves and seeds into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar.
Mix 1 part of the ground ginkgo biloba with 2 parts of 80 proof vodka in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter and store the herbal ginkgo biloba tincture in a dark bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chaste tree tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a natural compound called iridoid glycosides, which help to regulate hormonal imbalances that can lead to excessive bleeding. By balancing the body's estrogen and progesterone levels, chaste tree tincture can reduce the frequency and severity of heavy menstrual flows. Additionally, its anti-inflammatory properties may also help to ease discomfort and cramping associated with heavy periods, providing a natural and holistic approach to managing menstrual symptoms.
remedy_properties: Estrogen-regulating properties: Chaste tree tincture helps regulate estrogen levels, which can help reduce heavy bleeding by balancing hormone fluctuations.

Anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects: The tincture's anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties can help soothe the uterine lining and reduce inflammation, alleviating cramps and heavy bleeding associated with menstruation.
Oxytocin-modulating properties: Chaste tree tincture may also modulate oxytocin levels, a hormone involved in uterine contractions, which can help reduce excessive menstrual flow.
remedy_constituents: Iridoid Glycosides: These compounds are responsible for the anti-spasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to regulate uterine contractions and reduce excessive bleeding during menstruation.

Vitexin: This flavonoid has been shown to have a direct effect on the uterus, helping to regulate hormonal balance and reduce uterine contractions, which in turn helps to alleviate excessive bleeding.

Agnuside: This iridoid glycoside has been found to have anti-prolactin effects, which helps to regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly an increase in prolactin levels, that can lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in flavonoids and terpenoids, which are believed to have a regulating effect on the menstrual cycle and uterine contractions.
Seeds: They contain a higher concentration of aucubin, a compound that helps to reduce uterine contractions and alleviate excessive bleeding.
Leaves: They are also a good source of flavonoids and terpenoids, which contribute to their potential in regulating the menstrual cycle and reducing excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of dried vitex agnus castus flowers in the full bloom stage.
Combine the dried flowers with 50% ethanol in a 1:5 ratio in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Transfer the tincture to small glass bottles and label them, ready for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guelder rose tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich composition of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These bioactive compounds have been traditionally used to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate symptoms associated with heavy bleeding. The astringent properties of guelder rose help to contract blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding, while its anti-inflammatory effects soothe the uterine lining, promoting a healthy balance of hormones. As a result, guelder rose tinctures can provide relief from the distress and discomfort caused by over bleeding during periods.
remedy_properties: Vasoprotective and anti-inflammatory effects: The flavonoids present in Guelder Rose tincture help to protect blood vessels from damage, reducing inflammation and congestion that can lead to heavy bleeding during menstruation.

Antioxidant activity: Guelder Rose's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and tissue damage, which may exacerbate heavy bleeding during periods.

Hormone-regulating effects: Guelder Rose has been traditionally used to support the female reproductive system and regulate hormonal imbalances, which may help to reduce heavy menstrual bleeding by normalizing uterine contractions and blood flow.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: Tannins help with over bleeding during periods by contracting the uterine muscles and reducing blood loss through astringent properties.
Viburnin (Alkaloid): Viburnin, an alkaloid found in Viburnum opulus, has a hemostatic effect, which helps to stop excessive bleeding by promoting coagulation and reducing the loss of blood.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as ferulic acid, exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help reduce menstrual bleeding by alleviating inflammation and oxidative stress in the uterus.
remedy_parts: Fruits: They are used due to their astringent properties, which help to stop excessive bleeding.
Leaves: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce menstrual flow and alleviate cramps.
Roots: They are used due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to stop excessive bleeding and reduce menstrual cramps.
remedy_recipe: Gather fresh viburnum opulus flowers and leaves in equal parts and dry them for at least 24 hours.
Combine 250 grams of dried herb with 1 liter of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Label and store the viburnum opulus tincture in a cool dark place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Black cohosh tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique combination of phytoestrogens that can help regulate hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen dominance. This can lead to a reduction in heavy menstrual flow and clotting, providing relief from discomfort and anxiety associated with excessive bleeding. By stabilizing hormone levels, black cohosh tinctures may also alleviate cramping and mood swings, promoting a more balanced and comfortable menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic compounds: Black cohosh contains estrogenic compounds that can help to regulate menstrual bleeding and reduce excessive uterine contractions, leading to lighter and more manageable periods.

Vasopressin-like effects: Black cohosh has been shown to have vasopressin-like effects, which means it can help to constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow, thereby reducing heavy or prolonged menstrual bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in black cohosh may help to reduce uterine inflammation and swelling, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
remedy_constituents: Actein: It helps reduce uterine contractions and inflammation, which may contribute to excessive menstrual bleeding.
Phenolic glycosides (including acteoside): These compounds have a mild astringent effect, which may help reduce blood flow and alleviate excessive bleeding.
Triterpenoid saponins: They have a potential anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effect, which may help reduce uterine contractions and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a high concentration of actein, a saponin responsible for the plant's antispasmodic and astringent properties.
Rhyzomes: Similar to the roots, they also contain actein, which helps to reduce bleeding and alleviate cramping.
Barks: The barks of Actaea racemosa contain tannins, which help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried actaea racemosa roots and clean them thoroughly under cold running water.
Chop the roots into small pieces about one inch in length to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 80 proof vodka in a 1:5 ratio by volume for a strong tincture.
Steep the mixture in a glass jar with a lid in a cool dark place for four weeks.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another glass container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique blend of herbal compounds that help to regulate and balance the body's hormone production. The tincture's primary active ingredient, Angelica sinensis, is known for its ability to strengthen and tone the uterine lining, reducing excessive blood flow and promoting normal menstrual cycles. remedy_constituents: Ligustilide: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to reduce uterine contractions and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Angelica sinensis are rich in ferulic acid, which helps to reduce bleeding and alleviate menstrual cramps.

remedy_recipe: Gather 1 part angelica sinensis roots and stems and 2 parts vodka in a clean glass jar.
Combine the angelica sinensis mixture and vodka in the jar and seal the lid tightly.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 2 to 6 weeks to allow infusion.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its rich content of isoflavones, specifically biochanin A and formononetin. These compounds have been shown to help regulate hormonal balance, particularly estrogen levels, which can contribute to heavy menstrual flow. The tincture's ability to stabilize blood clotting factors and reduce inflammation also helps to alleviate bleeding issues. remedy_properties: Estrogenic effects: Red clover contains isoflavones, which can help to balance and regulate estrogen levels in the body, reducing heavy menstrual bleeding by promoting uterine health and slowing down blood flow.

Vasoprotective properties: The saponins present in red clover tincture have been shown to strengthen and stabilize blood vessels, which can help to reduce bleeding by preventing capillaries from breaking and leaking excessively.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Red clover's flavonoids and phenolic acids exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which can help to reduce prostaglandins (hormones that cause uterine contractions and bleeding) and alleviate symptoms of heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Isoflavones: These plant-derived estrogenic compounds help regulate menstrual cycles and reduce heavy bleeding by exerting a gentle estrogenic effect on the uterine lining.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which help to regulate menstrual cycles and alleviate heavy bleeding.
Leaves: Contain isoflavones and other bioactive compounds that help to ease menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 part of fresh trifolium pratense flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers by spreading them out in a single layer for 2 weeks.
Use a 1:5 ratio of trifolium pratense to 95% ethanol to create a strong tincture.
Steep the plant material in the solvent for 2-6 weeks in a well-sealed glass container.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean glass container and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a natural ability to reduce inflammation and slow down blood flow. The menthol in peppermint acts as a vasoconstrictor, constricting blood vessels and thereby reducing the amount of blood lost during menstruation. Additionally, peppermint's anti-inflammatory properties help to soothe the uterine lining, further alleviating heavy bleeding. Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can cause excessive bleeding by thinning the lining of the womb, thereby promoting a more balanced menstrual cycle.

Antioxidant and vasodilatory effects: The antioxidants in peppermint tincture help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by heavy menstruation, while its vasodilatory properties widen blood vessels, reducing blood pressure and thus easing bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with over bleeding during periods by reducing inflammation in the uterus, thereby decreasing bleeding.

Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid helps with over bleeding during periods by acting as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, which reduces oxidative stress and inflammation that can contribute to excessive bleeding.
Caryophyllene oxide: Caryophyllene oxide helps with over bleeding during periods by inhibiting platelet aggregation and promoting blood clotting, which can help to reduce excessive bleeding.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make tinctures for over bleeding during periods because they contain compounds that can help to reduce inflammation and ease menstrual cramps.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers when in full bloom during warm sunny days.
Chop the fresh plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for tincture extraction.
Combine 250 grams of chopped plant material with 750 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and steep for 2 to 3 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Evening primrose tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its unique ability to support uterine health. The rich source of gamma-linolenic acid (GLA) in herbal evening primrose oil helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy blood flow, thus alleviating excessive menstrual bleeding. Additionally, GLA has been shown to regulate prostaglandins, which play a crucial role in menstruation. By balancing these hormones, the tincture can help to normalize uterine contractions and minimize heavy bleeding associated with PMS.
remedy_properties: Gamma-Linolenic Acid (GLA): GLA, an omega-6 fatty acid, helps to regulate prostaglandins in the body, which can reduce inflammation and tone down uterine contractions, thereby reducing excessive bleeding.

Vitex Agnus-Castus (Chaste Tree): Vitex has a regulatory effect on the hormonal balance in the body, particularly the prostaglandin levels, which can help to normalize uterine bleeding patterns and reduce heavy menstrual flow.

Cytokinin: Cytokinin, a plant hormone found in evening primrose, helps to regulate cell division and growth, which can aid in reducing inflammation and tissue damage that may contribute to excessive bleeding.
remedy_constituents: Phenylethanoid glycosides: These glycosides in Oenothera biennis tinctures have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which may help reduce menstrual cramps and alleviate bleeding.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in Oenothera biennis tinctures have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help stabilize and regulate menstrual flow, potentially reducing excessive bleeding.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Oenothera biennis are used to make tinctures for over bleeding during periods due to their astringent properties, which help to reduce excessive menstrual flow.

Stems: The stems of Oenothera biennis are used to make tinctures for over bleeding during periods due to their astringent and antispasmodic properties, which help to ease menstrual cramps and reduce bleeding.

Leaves: The leaves of Oenothera biennis are used to make tinctures for over bleeding during periods due to their astringent properties, which help to tone the uterus and reduce excessive menstrual flow.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh oenothera biennis flowers and leaves in the morning when dew is still present.
Clean and dry the harvested plant material by spreading it out on paper bags for 1 week.
Steep 1 cup of the dried plant material in 2 cups of 80 proof vodka in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks.
Strain the liquid mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Label and store the tincture in a cool dark place for at least 2 months before using.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh capsicum annuum peppers when they are fully ripe and have reached their optimal color.
Chop 250 grams of fresh capsicum annuum peppers into small pieces to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped peppers with 500 milliliters of 80% ethanol in a clean glass container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because its anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce inflammation in the uterus, which can cause excessive bleeding. remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh zingiber officinale roots weighing 500 grams for optimal extraction properties.
Chop the harvested roots into small pieces and soak them in 1000 milliliters of vodka at 20% proof for 14 days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the liquid from the solids after 14 days have passed.
Transfer the extracted liquid to dark glass bottles and store it in a cool place for up to 6 months without exposure to direct sunlight.
Take 20-30 drops of the tincture thrice daily as needed, diluted with water or added to herbal teas for effective treatment of excessive bleeding during menstruation.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried withania somnifera root powder weighing approximately 250 grams.
Combine the withania somnifera root powder with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:3 by weight.
Store the mixture in a clean glass jar and let it macerate for 14 days allowing optimal extraction.

Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction, then strain through cheesecloth into another container.

remedy_parts: Roots: Licorice roots have been traditionally used to treat menstrual cramps and heavy bleeding due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, also known as the underground stem of the licorice plant, contains compounds that help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce uterine contractions.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of glycyrrhiza glabra in quantities of 100 grams.
Coarsely chop the harvested roots into small pieces using scissors or knife.
Combine the chopped roots with 70% ethanol in a ratio of 1:4 by weight for 48 hours maceration time.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or muslin cloth to remove solid residue and filter the liquid through paper filters.
Bottle the filtered tincture and store it in dark glass containers at room temperature for up to two years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it contains a unique compound called thujone, which has been shown to have potent antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. When taken as a supplement, wormwood tincture can help to reduce inflammation and congestion in the pelvic area, allowing for smoother blood flow and reducing excessive bleeding. Additionally, its ability to tone the uterine muscles may also help to regulate menstrual flow and alleviate cramps associated with heavy bleeding.
remedy_properties: Vasoconstrictive Properties: Wormwood tincture helps to constrict blood vessels, reducing blood flow and thus alleviating excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Anti-inflammatory Properties: Wormwood's anti-inflammatory compounds may help reduce inflammation in the uterus and cervix, which can contribute to heavy or prolonged bleeding.
Astringent Properties: Wormwood's astringent properties have a drying effect on tissues, which can help to stop or slow down bleeding by reducing blood flow and promoting clotting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are often used due to their rich concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which help to reduce inflammation and constrict blood vessels, slowing down heavy bleeding.
Barks: The barks are utilized for their ability to stimulate the uterus and tone the uterine muscles, thereby reducing excessive bleeding and promoting menstrual regularity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers.
Chop 500 grams of dried herbs into small pieces for infusion.
Steep chopped herbs in 1000 milliliters of vodka at room temperature for 2 weeks.
Strain and discard solids then transfer liquid to dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.
Store tincture in a cool dark place for 6 months to allow flavors to mature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it has a natural ability to regulate hormonal imbalances in the body. The tincture's active compounds, such as beta-sitosterol and fatty acids, work to normalize estrogen levels, which can help reduce heavy bleeding and alleviate symptoms of endometriosis. By promoting a healthy balance of hormones, saw palmetto tinctures can help shorten or stop excessive bleeding, providing relief from discomfort and allowing women to manage their menstrual cycles with greater ease.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Saw palmetto's antioxidant compounds help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to heavy menstrual bleeding.

Prostaglandin-regulating properties: Saw palmetto has been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that stimulate uterine contractions and may lead to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Serotonin-boosting properties: Saw palmetto's serotonin-boosting effects can help regulate blood vessels and platelet function, leading to reduced menstrual bleeding and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the saw palmetto plant contain natural astringent properties that help to constrict blood vessels and reduce excessive bleeding.
Fruits: The berries of the saw palmetto plant, which are actually modified fruits, contain fatty acids and sterols that help to regulate hormonal balance and alleviate heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried serenoa repens leaves and stems measuring about one pound.
Chop the herbs into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar with a lid.
Fill the jar to the top with vodka 95% ethanol as the menstruum at a ratio of 1:4.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for four weeks or forty days allowing the mixture to steep undisturbed.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass bottle and store it in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it balances hormones, regulating the body's natural rhythms. The herbal extract contains phytochemicals that help stabilize estrogen and progesterone levels, reducing inflammation and promoting uterine health. By normalizing hormone production, maca tincture can alleviate heavy menstrual flow, cramping, and mood swings associated with hormonal imbalance. As a result, women can experience more manageable and predictable periods, reducing anxiety and discomfort caused by excessive bleeding.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Maca tincture's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding by promoting vasoconstriction, thus alleviating heavy menstrual flow.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Maca's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce inflammation in the uterus and surrounding tissues, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during periods.

Hormone-balancing properties: Maca tincture is believed to have a regulating effect on hormonal imbalances, particularly estrogen-related issues, which can cause irregular or heavy menstrual bleeding.
remedy_parts: Roots: Maca roots are rich in starch, proteins, and other nutrients that help regulate hormonal imbalances and reduce heavy menstrual bleeding.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, or underground stem, of the maca plant contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and stabilize menstrual cramps and bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lepidium meyenii root powder from trusted sources.
Mix one part of the powdered root with two parts of vodka or ethanol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 4 to 6 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding the solids and preserving the liquid.
Transfer the filtered tincture into small brown glass bottles with droppers and label them clearly with dates and ingredients.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese thoroughwort tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because it has natural anticoagulant properties that help to regulate menstrual flow. The herb contains compounds that slow down blood clotting, allowing the body to heal and repair the uterine lining more efficiently. This can help to reduce excessive bleeding and heavy clots, making periods more manageable and comfortable for women. Additionally, Chinese thoroughwort has anti-inflammatory properties that may also contribute to its ability to alleviate period symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lei Gong Teng tincture helps reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the uterus, which can contribute to excessive bleeding during menstruation.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture's anti-inflammatory effects can help reduce uterine contractions and alleviate cramps associated with heavy menstrual bleeding.

Hemostatic properties: Lei Gong Teng contains compounds that can help stimulate blood clotting and platelet aggregation, which can help stop excessive bleeding by promoting hemostasis (the process of forming clots to stop bleeding).
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help to stop excessive uterine bleeding by promoting blood coagulation and reducing inflammation.
Rhyzomes: Contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce heavy menstrual flow by alleviating pain and swelling associated with endometriosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bupleurum falcatum roots in the morning after sunrise at room temperature.
Grind 200g of the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine the powdered roots with 500ml of vodka or high-proof grain alcohol in a glass jar, mixing well to ensure uniform coverage.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place at room temperature for 2 weeks, shaking the jar daily for 10 minutes.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another glass jar, discarding the solids and reserving the tincture for use within 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley tinctures helps with over bleeding during periods because of its natural ability to regulate blood flow and reduce inflammation. The herb's essential oils, such as apiol and myristicin, have a uterine contracting effect, which helps to slow down excessive menstrual bleeding. Additionally, parsley's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and calm the uterus, reducing cramping and discomfort associated with heavy periods. By using herbal parsley tinctures, women can find natural relief from the physical and emotional challenges of over bleeding during their menstrual cycle.
remedy_properties: Antihemorrhagic properties: Parsley contains flavonoids and carotenoids that have been shown to reduce blood loss by stimulating clotting factors and improving platelet function, helping to slow down menstrual flow.

Astringent properties: The tannins present in parsley tincture can help constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding by forming a protective film on the lining of the uterus, effectively "staunching" the flow of blood.

Antioxidant properties: Parsley's antioxidant compounds, such as vitamin C and beta-carotene, can help protect the body from oxidative stress and inflammation associated with heavy menstrual bleeding, promoting overall uterine health and reducing bleeding intensity.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in apigenin, a flavonoid with anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which helps to reduce excessive bleeding.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of apiol, a volatile oil that acts as a stimulant and can help to constrict blood vessels, reducing excessive bleeding.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and clean petroselinum crispum leaves according to organic farming standards within one week of flowering for optimal potency.
Chop the fresh leaves into small pieces and combine with 70% vodka in a ratio of 1:3 by weight for proper extraction.
Place the leaf-vodka mixture into glass jars or containers and seal them tightly allowing for steeping periods of 4-6 weeks at room temperature.
After the steeping period strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate solids from liquids discarding the solids.
Transfer the final tincture to dark amber glass bottles filled to capacity with no headspace and store them in a cool dark place for a minimum of 2 years before use.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to over bleeding during periods that are treated with herbal tinctures are various uterine and gynecological issues. These include menorrhagia, dysmenorrhea, endometriosis, fibroids, and polycystic ovary syndrome (PCOS). Herbal tinctures like Angelica, Ashwagandha, Chamomile, Dong Quai, and Ginger have been used to ease symptoms such as heavy bleeding, cramping, and hormonal imbalances. They also help regulate the menstrual cycle and alleviate PMS symptoms.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal tinctures that help with over bleeding during periods is a blend of Red Clover, Wild Yams, and Blue Cohosh. Red Clover helps to tone the uterus and reduce bleeding, while Wild Yams support hormone balance and reduce cramping. Blue Cohosh, with its anti-inflammatory properties, helps to constrict blood vessels and reduce heavy bleeding. Taking these tinctures individually or in combination under the guidance of a healthcare professional can help alleviate excessive bleeding and promote overall menstrual health.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Current Drug Discovery Technologies, herbal tinctures can be beneficial for over bleeding during periods due to their ability to induce regular bleeding patterns. The study found that in women with oligomenorrhea and secondary amenorrhea, the herbal tea Fomentex, which is a blend of Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha longifolia, and Vitex agnus-castus, induced bleeding in 83.3% of women in the first cycle and maintained regular bleeding patterns in 50% of women in the next (drug-free) episode, compared to 94.1% and 23.5% in the MP group, respectively. Additionally, the study showed that Fomentex reduced secondary outcomes such as dysmenorrhea, acne, and hirsutism, while also being safe and well-tolerated.
